name: Ensure API keys are not created for a project
body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **aPI keys are not created for a project** across AWS and Google Cloud.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment focuses on aPI keys that are created for a project. It reviews the identity, credential, role, or policy state that creates the access condition described by the control.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.

This control maps to a benchmark-style requirement, so the expected state should match the benchmark wording unless your organization has an approved exception.
description: API keys that are created for a project
remediationDescription:
## GCP remediation
+
+### Console
+1. Open APIs and Services -> Credentials.
+2. Identify and remove unnecessary API keys.
+3. For keys that must exist temporarily, restrict by API, application, and source.
+4. Prefer service accounts and workload identity over API keys.
+
+### Google Cloud CLI
+List API keys in a project:
+
+~~~bash
+gcloud services api-keys list --project <project-id>
+~~~
+
+Delete an API key:
+
+~~~bash
+gcloud services api-keys delete <key-id> --project <project-id>
+~~~
+
+### References
+- https://cloud.google.com/api-keys/docs
+- https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/services/api-keys
